chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: Participants are rapidly generating new ideas by drawing analogies from various adjacent fields (heterogeneous catalysis, batteries, photocatalysis) to expand the possibilities for electrochemistry.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Visible participants (Jordi, Venkat) show consistent engagement through head nods and shared smiles, though some participants remain off-camera or relatively still.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Connecting battery SEI concepts to electrosynthesis.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group has narrowed the domain to complex interfaces, hybrid driving forces, and cross-scale modeling, but has not yet articulated a specific research question.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The discussion remains in an open, exploratory phase with multiple distinct ideas being proposed without narrowing down to a single project.
chunk_summary.skill_gap_description: Wilson Smith identifies a gap in the field's ability to perform continuum modeling of electrochemical systems across different length and time scales.
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group is highly generative, drawing analogies from adjacent fields (batteries, heterogeneous catalysis, photocatalysis) to identify blind spots in electrochemistry, culminating in Jordi's synthesis that they are all gravitating towards 'complexity'.

ideas_currently_on_table: Accelerating electrochemistry discovery using machine learning and robotics
Learning from heterogeneous and photocatalysis for extreme conditions and local redox
Applying battery SEI concepts to tune electrosynthesis
Continuum modeling of electrochemical systems across scales
chunk_summaries: Emily proposed using city sensor networks for CO2 measurement and Alissa raised the possibility of extending this to indoor air capture; Emily drove most of the ideation while Alissa asked clarifying questions. The tone was collaborative with visible head nods and active backchannels. The group ended the chunk in early divergent exploration with no specific project named.

The group engaged in a highly divergent brainstorming session, drawing analogies from adjacent fields to identify new frontiers for electrochemistry. Shu Hu proposed learning from heterogeneous catalysis and corrosion, Kelsey Hatzell introduced thermo-electrochemistry, and Venkat Viswanathan suggested applying battery SEI concepts to electrosynthesis. Jordi Cabana synthesized these diverse ideas by noting a shared gravitation toward 'complexity' and cascade reactions. The tone was collaborative and intellectually playful, highlighted by a humorous exchange between Jordi and Venkat about the battery community. The chunk ended with the group still in an open exploratory phase, identifying gaps like the need for continuum modeling.
